column1: It was Sunday morning. James_____________a book about insects when his elder sister Beth came into the room. (READ)
Beth____________James and said, "Oh, James, do you know where my jacket is? I can't find it." (SEE)
She thought she had left it_____________in the wardrobe in her room, butwhen she looked, it wasn't there. (HANG)
James decided to play a little game with Beth. "I_____________you if you agree to answer this question," he said. (TELL)
Beth was annoyed and she_____________like playing games, but she agreed anyway, and asked what the question was. (NOT FEEL)
Smiling James asked her how many__________a centipede has. (FOOT)
"Oh, you mean_____________long scary insects that I hate," Beth said. "I don't know how many they have. Is it 100?" she guessed. (THAT)
"The answer is none - they've only got legs! And they've got between 20 and 300 so even if you___________100 legs, you would have been wrong," James told her. (SAY)
Beth told James that that was the___________thing she'd ever heard. "Now, tell me where my jacket is!" said Beth. (SILLY)
The Imperial Academy of Arts is in St Petersburg, Russia. Since it opened, its tutors____________many great artists. (TEACH)
It____________in 1757 by the Russian Minister of Education at the time, Ivan Shuvalov. (CREATE)
The building _____________is grand and beautiful. It was built in the classical style and is on the banks of the Neva River. (IT)
In its early years, if students wanted to have successful ___________as artists, they had to attend this school. (LIFE)
Back then, the_____________students at the Academy often travelled to Europe to study different styles of art. (GOOD)
The Academy ______________to have a great deal of art, but now most of it has been moved to the Hermitage Museum in St Petersburg. (USE)
Officials______________it a few times since 1757. Officially, it is now known as the Ilya Repin St Petersburg State Academic Institute for Painting, Sculpture and Architecture. (RENAME)
Ilya Repin is one of Russia's greatest artists. He_________to the Imperial Academy of Arts in the 1870s. (GO)
His____________work is the painting Ivan the Terrible and His Son. (FAMOUS)
